name: com.wayn.games.locomotion
description:
The Locomotion Package provides a modular, ECS-based architecture for implementing character control in Unity using state-driven locomotion logic. It cleanly separates gameplay entities—Players, Characters, and Models—and handles input, movement, and visual synchronization in a scalable and maintainable way.

It integrates seamlessly with Unity's New Input System and Cinemachine, but remains flexible enough to support custom input or camera solutions.

samples.description: Common resources and utility scripts used across all samples.
An introductory example showcasing basic locomotion in a single-player setup, using the legacy Input System with custom scripted camera controls.
A demonstration of local multiplayer gameplay built with the Input System package and Cinemachine, using a shared locomotion state machine.
An example of a point-and-click style controller that allows switching between multiple playable characters.
A platforming-focused example featuring platform interactions, arbitrary gravity, character animations, and advanced moves such as dashing and climbing.
